segments.text: Abraham was now very old, and the Lord had blessed him in every way.
He said to the senior servant in his household, the one in charge of all that he had,
Put your hand under my thigh.
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: I want you to swear by the Lord, the God of heaven and the God of earth, that you will not get a wife for my son from the daughters of the Canaanites, among whom I am living,
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: but will go to my country and my own relatives and get a wife for my son Isaac.
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: The servant asked him,
What if the woman is unwilling to come back with me to this land? Shall I then take your son back to the country you came from?
segments.speaker: Servant

segments.text: Abraham said,
Make sure that you do not take my son back there.
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: The Lord, the God of heaven, who brought me out of my father's household and my native land and who spoke to me and promised me on oath, saying, 'To your offspring I will give this land'—he will send his angel before you so that you can get a wife for my son from there.
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: If the woman is unwilling to come back with you, then you will be released from this oath of mine. Only do not take my son back there.
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: So the servant put his hand under the thigh of his master Abraham and swore an oath to him concerning this matter.
Lord, God of my master Abraham, make me successful today, and show kindness to my master Abraham.
segments.speaker: Servant

segments.text: The servant hurried to meet her and said,
Please give me a little water from your jar.
segments.speaker: Servant

segments.text: Drink, my lord,
segments.speaker: Rebekah

segments.text: and quickly lowered the jar to her hands and gave him a drink.
Then the man bowed down and worshiped the Lord,

Praise be to the Lord, the God of my master Abraham, who has not abandoned his kindness and faithfulness to my master. As for me, the Lord has led me on the journey to the house of my master's relatives.
segments.speaker: Servant

segments.text: Come, you who are blessed by the Lord. Why are you standing out here? I have prepared the house and a place for the camels.
segments.speaker: Laban

segments.text: Then food was set before him, but he said,
I will not eat until I have told you what I have to say.
segments.speaker: Servant

segments.text: Laban said,
Then tell us.
segments.speaker: Laban

segments.text: This is from the Lord; we can say nothing to you one way or the other.
segments.speaker: Laban and Bethuel

segments.text: Here is Rebekah; take her and go, and let her become the wife of your master's son, as the Lord has directed.
segments.speaker: Laban and Bethuel

segments.text: So they called Rebekah and asked her,
Will you go with this man?
segments.speaker: Laban and family

segments.text: I will go.
segments.speaker: Rebekah

segments.text: Our sister, may you increase to thousands upon thousands; may your offspring possess the cities of their enemies.
segments.speaker: Laban and family

segments.text: She asked the servant,
Who is that man in the field coming to meet us?
segments.speaker: Rebekah

segments.text: The servant answered,
He is my master.
segments.speaker: Servant
